title: Superconductivity near quarter- and half-filling of a strongly correlated triangular Hubbard band in twisted trilayer WSe2
authors: Yungi Jeong, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Joonho Jang
abstract: The triangular-lattice Hubbard model is one of the paradigmatic settings for studying correlated electronic systems with geometric frustration that can give rise to a variety of phases. Recently, twisted bilayer TMD systems have emerged as attractive experimental platforms for realising such a model in the moderate-correlation regime. Alternating twisted trilayer TMD systems are expected to realise stronger correlations than bilayer systems owing to their mirror symmetry. Here we report the discovery of superconductivity near quarter- and half-filling, together with various correlated phases, in alternating twisted trilayer WSe2 (TTWSe2). At half filling, a robust correlated insulating state persists over a broad range of displacement fields, signalling the strongly correlated regime. Upon doping, superconductivity emerges over an extended region of the phase diagram. Near quarter filling, another superconducting state appears flanked by correlated metals. Our results establish the TTWSe2 system as a compelling platform for studying strongly correlated triangular-lattice Hubbard physics.
summary: The paper reports on the discovery and characterization of superconductivity near quarter- and half-filling in twisted trilayer WSe2. By combining transport measurements with continuum theoretical models, the authors map out a complex phase diagram featuring Mott insulators and superconducting domes. This establishes the system as a crucial platform for investigating strongly correlated triangular-lattice Hubbard physics.
main_problem: The study investigates superconductivity and various correlated insulating phases occurring near quarter- and half-filling in a strongly correlated triangular Hubbard band system.
main_result: Superconductivity is observed over extended regions of the phase diagram, alongside robust Mott insulators at half-filling and other correlated insulating states at quarter-filling.
method: The research combines electrical transport measurements (resistance vs. filling/field) with theoretical continuum model calculations to map out the complex phase diagram.
model_or_system: The physical platform is alternating twisted trilayer WSe2, which realizes a system analogous to the triangular-lattice Hubbard model. The analysis uses continuum $k\cdot p$ frameworks and calculates susceptibilities for weak-coupling instability analysis.
key_observables: Longitudinal resistance (R), superconducting transition regions ($	ext{SC}1, 	ext{SC}2$), Mott insulating states ($	ext{Mott}1, 	ext{Mott}2$), and characteristic temperature dependencies ($T_{	ext{coh}}, T'$).
important_parameters:
Filling factor ($
u$) near $1/4$ and $1/2$, displacement field ($D$), magnetic field ($B$), and the effective on-site interaction ($U$).
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on continuum descriptions, assuming strong spin-orbit coupling leads to effective spin-valley locking, and uses simplified treatments for parameters like the effective g-factor.
figures_summary: Figures detail phase diagrams mapping superconducting domes adjacent to insulating states, showing field dependence of superconductivity, and comparing transport measurements against calculated electronic structure features (DOS, Berry curvature).
paper_structure:
The paper systematically maps out a rich phase diagram by performing transport measurements across varying doping ($
u$) and electric fields ($D$), correlating the observed superconducting and insulating regimes with theoretical predictions derived from continuum models of the triangular Hubbard model.
why_interesting: This work provides an experimental realization platform for studying fundamental strongly correlated physics (Hubbard model) in a solid-state moiré system, offering insights into unconventional superconductivity mechanisms driven by electronic correlations.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Fig. 1 | Electronic structure of alternating twisted trilayer WSe₂. a, Schematic illustration of the dual-gated TTWSe2 device and stacking geometry. The top and bottom WSe2 layers are aligned, while the middle layer is rotated by θ. b, Real-space structure of TTWSe2. Coloured dots mark representative local atomic registries, shown at right for the MXM, MMM and XMX configurations (M = W, X = Se). Each interface generates a moiré potential and contributions from each interface combine to produce an approximately doubled moiré modulation at the middle layer (bottom). c, Schematic illustration of momentum- space structure. Large hexagons indicate the Brillouin zone of the top (red), middle...

Fig. 2 | Correlated electronic states. a,c,d, Longitudinal resistance R as a function of hole filling factor ν and vertical displacement field D, measured at B = 0 T and T = 30 mK (a), 100 mK (c), and 1 K (d). b, Schematic phase diagram summarising the correlated states identified in a. Black curves separate the layer-polarised and layer-hybridised regimes. The coloured regions indicate superconducting states (blue; SC1), superconducting-like resistance drops with finite resistance (light blue; SC2), the ν = 1 Mott insulator (red; Mott1), an additional insulator on the electron side of ν = 1 (yellow; Mott2), a weaker correlated insulating state near ν = 1/2 (orange; Corr), generalised...

Fig. 3 | Superconductivity near half moiré filling. a, b, differential resistance dV/dI as a function of dc bias current I and T at B = 0 (a), I and B at T = 30 mK (b). c, Zero-bias resistance R versus T at B = 0 and D = 0. Upper-left inset, representative linecuts from a. Lower-right inset, corresponding I–V curves. The curve at TBKT ≈ 230 mK is shown as a dashed line in both insets. d, Zero-bias resistance as a function of T and B at D = 0.
